owner_intent: The account screenshots were collected to teach how each account should present its first screen and cover grid, not merely to collect isolated image styles.
rule: Design the first-screen experience as an account-level system before designing an individual post.
first_screen_components: account promise visible across the first nine tiles
stable cover typography and text position
repeatable image crop and subject hierarchy
controlled variation across photo, illustration, diagram, screenshot, and generated media
thumbnail readability before the post is opened
clear distinction from the other project accounts

cover_rules: purple, pink, cream, black, and neon accents
people, diagrams, UI, and platform icons mixed across the grid
reference_account_grammars.best_use: service, automation, promotion, and product-system content

cover_rules: visual genre may vary while text position stays fixed
reference_account_grammars.best_use: news, trends, technology, and visual discovery
analysis, professional knowledge, prompts, and workflow guidance

cover_rules: white text with yellow key result
central mascot, person, comparison, or output
tool icons used as fast context
reference_account_grammars.best_use: tutorials, tool comparisons, generated results, and creator tips
prompt packs, resource collections, skills, and step-by-step guides

cover_rules: white setup plus yellow punch phrase
reference_account_grammars.best_use: film, drama, conflict, rescue, and character backstory

cover_rules: recurring person in every main tile
white setup plus pink emotion phrase
reference_account_grammars.best_use: named-person entertainment, reactions, habits, relationships, and personality stories

application_rules: Select an account grammar before selecting a layout for an individual post.
Judge a candidate cover both alone and inside a simulated three-by-three first-screen grid.
Keep the account's typography, accent roles, and crop logic stable while varying topic, mood, and media.
Do not collapse all accounts into one universal cover style.
Do not copy a reference account literally; extract its system and adapt it to the project's own identity.
A strong single cover may still be rejected if it breaks the account's first-screen rhythm.
